news.title: Switzerland plans stricter rules for real estate purchases by foreigners
news.para: Switzerland is planning to enforce stricter regulations for foreign property buyers. The new measures will particularly impact non-EU/EFTA citizens, limiting their ability to purchase primary homes and commercial properties. This initiative seeks to tackle the ongoing housing crisis and curb rapid population growth.

news.title: Brigade Group to jointly develop 8.63 acres land in East Bengaluru
news.para: Brigade Group has secured a joint development agreement for a significant land parcel in Gunjur, East Bengaluru. This 8.63-acre plot is a key part of a larger 39-acre residential township. The entire project is estimated to have a gross development value of ₹7,200 crore.

news.title: Office vacancy declines to 13.85% in Q1 2026: Report
news.para: Sustained leasing activity and slower supply additions pushed office vacancy down to 13.85% in Q1 2026, with rentals crossing ₹100 per sq ft per month for the first time.

news.title: Behind the society gates lies India’s most valuable real estate story
news.para: In India, the trend of affluent consumers gravitating towards gated communities is on the rise. These self-sustained neighborhoods are not just residential areas; they are becoming vibrant economic hubs. By 2031, it's estimated that close to half of households in leading cities will call these structured environments home.

news.title: Adrianse rebrands as Design Alphabet, signalling a new era of integrated design
news.para: Adrianse has transformed into Design Alphabet, a bold rebranding that emphasizes the integral role of architecture in their progressive design philosophy. As the studio broadens its international footprint into the realms of commercial, healthcare, education, and research and development, Design Alphabet seamlessly merges architecture, interiors, and technology to enhance project clarity and execution.

news.title: Ludhiana improvement trust organises draw of lots for 179 Atal Apartment flats
news.para: Ludhiana Improvement Trust held a Baisakhi draw for 179 Atal Apartment flats, with Local Bodies Minister Sanjeev Arora present. Successful allottees were assured possession by year-end, as construction progresses rapidly. LIT also plans new affordable housing and commercial projects, with the government promising future schemes for those who missed out.

news.title: Notice issued to seven Dharavi slum settlements in Mumbai to vacate before rains
news.para: Residents of nearly seven slum settlements in Dharavi's Sector 6 have received notices to vacate. This land is designated for rehabilitation buildings under the Dharavi Redevelopment Project. Approximately 3,500 households must move to allow construction of 10 new buildings for 11,000 families.

news.title: PMC cracks down on 30 RMC plants in Pune in three months following residents’ complaints
news.para: Pune Municipal Corporation is intensifying its crackdown on ready-mix concrete plants following numerous citizen complaints about pollution and accident risks. The civic body has sealed and demolished several unauthorized facilities, with more actions planned. A policy to manage existing plants and potentially relocate them outside city limits is also under consideration.

news.title: Lucknow civic body plans to simplify property mutation charges
news.para: Lucknow Municipal Corporation plans to simplify property mutation charges. A new slab-based system will be introduced for property sales and ancestral transfers. This aims to bring transparency and reduce disputes. Charges for private establishments like hospitals and restaurants will also be fixed. The proposal will be presented at the LMC House meeting soon.

news.title: Real estate developers in Ludhiana seek action against illegal colonies
news.para: Local real estate developers are urging authorities to crack down on illegal colonies, citing financial losses for legitimate builders and unplanned urban growth. They highlight that these unauthorized developments bypass regulations, leading to a lack of amenities for buyers and revenue loss for the government. Developers demand stricter monitoring and penalties to prevent future issues.

news.title: Ashiana Housing to invest ₹800 crore for land acquisition in FY27
news.para: Ashiana Housing plans to invest ₹800 crore in land acquisition across India in FY27, a senior executive said. The company reported record senior living sales of ₹570.2 crore in FY26, driven by strong demand in the segment.

news.title: Surat civic body sends tax bills for EWS homes it demolished
news.para: Residents of Surat's demolished Pragya Nagar homes are bewildered as the Municipal Corporation demands property tax for properties razed a year ago. Evacuation notices were served two years back, yet bills continued to arrive even after demolition. Many displaced families face financial strain, struggling with rent and now taxes for non-existent homes.

news.title: Over 45,000 Nashik properties in use without building completion documents
news.para: As per data available with the civic body, the highest number of unauthorised properties — around 10,400 — are located in the Nashik Road division, followed by Panchavati with 9,800 such properties.

news.title: About 3.76 lakh owners skip paying property tax, Nagpur civic body's coffers hit
news.para: For 2025-26, the Nagpur Municipal Corporation's dept initially set a target of Rs350 crore, which was later revised upwards to Rs400 crore, further widening the gap between expectations and actual collections.

news.title: Drone-based land mapping boosts rural credit flows
news.para: Drone-based land mapping under the SVAMITVA scheme has boosted rural credit by 23% by converting residential plots into verifiable collateral. This formalization of land ownership, replacing informal demarcation with property cards, reduces lender risk and enables banks to extend credit more freely to previously excluded rural households.

news.title: Dharavi redevelopment gathers pace; 11,000 rehab homes planned on railway land
news.para: The Dharavi Redevelopment Project is accelerating, and residents in Sector-6 on railway land have been urged to cooperate and vacate homes. Construction of new rehabilitation buildings will begin soon.  Residents have been offered transit accommodation or rental support.

news.title: Karnataka to develop private-style layouts for SC/ST communities
news.para: Under the scheme, fully serviced layouts with complete infrastructure will be created and residential sites allotted to people from Scheduled Castes (SC) and Scheduled Tribes (ST) across the state.

news.title: About 19 big slum clusters across Mumbai set to undergo redevelopment under new scheme
news.para: Clusters of at least 50 acres, where over 51% of the area is occupied by slums, will be taken up for the projects. Even slums on coastal regulation zones I and II can be integrate

news.title: Haryana approves housing projects for 2,646 PMAY-Urban beneficiaries
news.para: Haryana government has approved housing projects for 2,646 beneficiaries across 60 urban local bodies under the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana-Urban (PMAY-U) 2.0. This initiative aims to provide affordable pucca houses to economically weaker sections, lower and middle-income groups. The state has seen significant public confidence with over 1.69 lakh registrations, and central funds have been released for thousands of homes.
